concerns.text: Task T029 instructs implementation to 'reject Ridge' if VIF > 5. This directly violates Spec FR-005 and FR-012 which mandate applying Ridge regression (lambda=1.0) as the fallback before switching to univariate models. The task silently weakens a functional requirement by implementing the Plan's methodological override instead of the Spec's constraint.

Task T032 defines the sensitivity analysis p-value sweep as '{0.05, 0.1}'. Spec FR-010 and US-3 explicitly require the sweep to include the empty set (uncorrected) alongside '{0.05, 0.1}'. The task omits the uncorrected baseline, failing to satisfy the full acceptance criterion for sensitivity analysis.

Task T015 implements motion exclusion but does not include the step to save the calculated Mean Framewise Displacement (FD) as a mandatory covariate for the statistical model. The Plan (Methodological Clarification point 3) and Spec FR-013 (confounders) imply FD must be available for the ANCOVA model. Omitting this step breaks the data flow required for the statistical analysis defined in US-3.

Task T013 tags FR-011 and FR-009 but the description only mentions checking for 'paired pre/post fMRI and clinical scores'. It omits the explicit requirement from FR-009 to verify the instrument is a 'validated anxiety scale with citable documentation' or halt. The task description is a semantic weakening of the requirement.
